title: C# Basic — Paper 4
description: C# concepts: type casting, operators, conditionals, switch expressions, loops, ref/out, classes, constructors, access modifiers, and properties.

questions.text: What is the difference between `Convert.ToInt32()` and `(int)` cast in C# when applied to a `double`?

options.text: Both truncate the decimal portion
`Convert.ToInt32()` rounds to nearest integer; `(int)` truncates toward zero
`(int)` rounds to nearest; `Convert.ToInt32()` truncates
There is no difference in the result

questions.text: What is the result of `(int)(10.9)` in C#?

options.text: Compile error

questions.text: When performing unboxing in C#, what must the target type exactly match?

options.text: The target type must be a parent type of the boxed value's type
The target type must exactly match the type originally boxed, or an InvalidCastException occurs
Any numeric type can receive any unboxed numeric value
The target type can be any compatible interface

questions.text: What is the result of `8 >> 1` (right shift by 1) in C#?
What does the compound assignment operator `+=` do in C#?

options.text: It adds and then assigns; `x += 3` is equivalent to `x = x + 3`
It appends to a collection
It increments by 1
It assigns the sum without modifying the variable

questions.text: What is the value of `int x = 3; int y = x++ + ++x;`?
What happens if you omit the `break` at the end of a non-empty `switch` case in C#?

options.text: Execution falls through to the next case automatically
A compile-time error is produced
The switch exits to the default case
The program throws a runtime exception

questions.text: What is the difference between a `switch` statement and a `switch` expression in C#?

options.text: A switch statement produces a value; a switch expression does not
A switch expression produces a value and uses `=>` arms; a switch statement uses `case/break` and executes side effects
They are identical in syntax and behavior
A switch expression requires a `default` arm; a switch statement does not

questions.text: Which is a valid nested if statement in C#?

options.text: if (x > 0) { if (x < 10) { Console.Write("single digit"); } }
if (x > 0 && { x < 10 }) { Console.Write("ok"); }
if (x > 0) then { if (x < 10) then {} }
nested if (x > 0) { (x < 10) }

questions.text: What does a switch expression arm using a type pattern `Shape s when s is Circle c => c.Area()` do in C#?

options.text: Matches only if the shape is a Circle AND the `when` guard is met; binds to `c`
Only checks the type without any guard condition
Throws an InvalidCastException if the shape is not a Circle
This syntax is invalid in C#

questions.text: What does the `is` pattern matching expression `obj is string s` do in C#?

options.text: Converts obj to string
Tests if obj is a string and if true, assigns it to variable s
Checks if s equals obj
Creates a new string variable s regardless of obj's type

questions.text: What is the output of a `for` loop `for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) Console.Write(i);`?

options.text: Infinite loop

questions.text: What is the key difference between a `while` loop and a `do-while` loop in C#?

options.text: A `while` loop always runs at least once; a `do-while` may run zero times
A `do-while` loop always runs at least once; a `while` may run zero times
They are identical in behavior
A `do-while` loop cannot use `break`

questions.text: What does `foreach` iterate over in C#?

options.text: Only arrays
Only List<T> collections
Any object that implements IEnumerable or IEnumerable<T>
Only numeric ranges

questions.text: In a nested loop, which statement does `break` exit from in C#?

options.text: The entire program
All loops at once
Only the innermost loop it is placed in
The outermost loop

questions.text: What happens to an `out` parameter if you call a method inline without pre-declaring the variable, using `Method(out int result)`?

options.text: Compile error — variables must be pre-declared
The variable `result` is declared and initialized by the method call
The variable is set to 0 automatically before the call
This syntax is not valid in C#

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `ref` keyword when passing a value type to a method?

options.text: It converts the value type to a reference type
It passes the variable by reference so the method can read and modify the original variable
It prevents the method from reading the parameter
It makes the parameter optional

questions.text: What does `in` do differently from `ref` when passed a struct to a method?

options.text: `in` passes a copy; `ref` passes by reference
`in` passes by reference but is read-only; `ref` passes by reference and allows modification
`in` is faster because it copies on write; `ref` always copies
They are identical for structs

questions.text: Which of the following is a valid class definition in C#?

options.text: object MyClass { string Name; }
class MyClass { public string Name; }
type MyClass = { Name: string }
define class MyClass { string Name; }

questions.text: What is the difference between a class field and a property in C#?

options.text: Fields have get/set accessors; properties are plain variables
Properties have get/set accessors allowing controlled access; fields are plain variables holding state
Properties are always public; fields are always private
There is no meaningful difference

questions.text: What does a copy constructor do in C#?

options.text: It is automatically called by the garbage collector
It creates a new object as a copy of an existing object, typically using an existing instance as parameter
It is a static method that returns a copy
It is a built-in C# constructor type

questions.text: What is the output of `new MyClass { X = 10, Y = 20 }` if `MyClass` has properties `X` and `Y`?

options.text: Compile error because no constructor was explicitly defined
An instance of MyClass is created with X set to 10 and Y set to 20
X and Y remain 0 because the default constructor runs
Runtime exception because X and Y must be set via constructor

questions.text: What is a static constructor used for in C#?

options.text: To initialize instance fields when each object is created
To initialize static fields of the class before the first use
To provide a public factory for object creation
To call parent class constructors

questions.text: What is the `private protected` access modifier in C#?

options.text: Accessible by the same class and any derived class in any assembly
Accessible within the same class and by derived classes within the SAME assembly only
Accessible only within the same class
Accessible within the same assembly regardless of inheritance

questions.text: Which access modifier provides the most restrictive access in C#?

options.text: protected
internal
private
private protected

questions.text: What is an auto-implemented property in C#?

options.text: A property generated by reflection at runtime
A property with `get` and `set` that has an automatically created backing field without explicit implementation
A property that calculates its value without storing it
A property that only has a getter

questions.text: What is an expression-bodied property in C#?

options.text: A property that uses a lambda to compute its get value: `public int Area => Width * Height;`
A property that has both get and set using lambdas
A property whose setter evaluates an expression
A property defined inside a method

questions.text: What does the `init` accessor on a property do in C# 9+?

options.text: It allows the property to be set only during object construction or object initializer syntax
It initializes the property to a default value
It makes the property read-only like `get` only
It calls the constructor when the property is first accessed

questions.text: What is a backing field for a property in C#?

options.text: A method that validates the property value
A private field that stores the value exposed by the property's getter and setter
A public field automatically created by the compiler
A secondary property that mirrors the primary

questions.text: Which of the following correctly defines a property with a backing field and validation?

options.text: public int Age { get; set; } = age >= 0 ? age : 0;
public int Age => _age;
private int _age; public int Age { get => _age; set => _age = value >= 0 ? value : 0; }
public int Age = { validate(age); return age; }
